questions.q: What kind of object is the Pushpaka Vimana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana is a self-moving aerial chariot that travels through the sky.

questions.q: Which demon-king seized the Pushpaka Vimana before the events of the war?
questions.explanation: Ravana took the Pushpaka Vimana by force and used it as his own.

questions.q: After Ravana's defeat, the Pushpaka Vimana carried Rama and his companions home to which city?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana bore Rama, Sita, and their companions back to Ayodhya.

questions.q: Through what medium does the Pushpaka Vimana travel?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana is an aerial vehicle that moves through the air.

questions.q: Which princess of Videha returned to Ayodhya aboard the Pushpaka Vimana with Rama?
questions.explanation: Sita, rescued from Lanka, flew home with Rama aboard the Pushpaka Vimana.

questions.q: A notable trait of the Pushpaka Vimana is that it is described as self-moving, meaning it can do what?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana is famed as a self-moving aerial chariot that obeys its master's wish.

questions.q: In which kingdom was the Pushpaka Vimana based while Ravana possessed it?
questions.explanation: Ravana kept the Pushpaka Vimana in his island kingdom of Lanka.

questions.q: The Pushpaka Vimana is best classified as which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana is a wondrous celestial vehicle rather than a weapon.

questions.q: The Sanskrit word 'vimana' in 'Pushpaka Vimana' most nearly refers to what?
questions.explanation: 'Vimana' denotes a celestial chariot or flying vehicle in Sanskrit tradition.

questions.q: Whose return journey to Ayodhya after the war was made aboard the Pushpaka Vimana?
questions.explanation: Rama, victorious over Ravana, traveled home to Ayodhya in the Pushpaka Vimana.

questions.q: Who originally created the Pushpaka Vimana, according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the divine architect Vishvakarma fashioned the Pushpaka for Brahma.

questions.q: From whom did Ravana take the Pushpaka Vimana by force?
questions.explanation: Ravana seized the Pushpaka from his half-brother Kubera, the lord of wealth.

questions.q: After Ravana's death, to whom did Rama give the Pushpaka Vimana as the rightful owner?
questions.explanation: Rama installed Vibhishana as king of Lanka and the Pushpaka was placed at his command before the journey home.

questions.q: Which god originally received the Pushpaka Vimana from Brahma before it passed to Kubera?
questions.explanation: Brahma bestowed the Pushpaka on Kubera, the god of riches, from whom Ravana later took it.

questions.q: A remarkable feature of the Pushpaka described in the epic is that it could do what according to the needs of its rider?
options: Expand or change size as required
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka was said to expand to accommodate however many passengers boarded it, never becoming crowded.

questions.q: Which loyal devotee of Rama also boarded the Pushpaka Vimana for the journey from Lanka to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, along with other vanara and rakshasa allies, accompanied Rama aboard the Pushpaka.

questions.q: On the homeward flight, Rama pointed out to Sita the sites of which past event below?
options: The forge where the bow of Shiva was made
The mountain where Hanuman was born
questions.explanation: From the Pushpaka, Rama showed Sita the battlefield of Lanka and other places they had passed during their ordeal.

questions.q: Why was the return to Ayodhya aboard the Pushpaka especially timely for Rama?
options: To arrive before his fourteen-year exile ended
To deliver a message before dawn
questions.explanation: Rama needed to reach Ayodhya as his fourteen-year exile concluded, and the swift Pushpaka made this possible.

questions.q: On the way to Ayodhya, the Pushpaka stopped at the hermitage of which sage so Rama could pay respects?
questions.explanation: Rama halted at the ashram of the sage Bharadvaja near Prayaga during the return journey.

questions.q: Whom did Rama send ahead from Bharadvaja's hermitage to announce his coming to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama dispatched Hanuman ahead to inform Bharata of his imminent return.

questions.q: The 'Pushpaka' name is connected to which of the following images?
questions.explanation: The name 'Pushpaka' is associated with flowers ('pushpa'), and the chariot is described as flower-adorned.

questions.q: Which brother greeted Rama and had waited as regent during the exile, after the Pushpaka brought Rama home?
questions.explanation: Bharata, who had ruled as Rama's regent placing his sandals on the throne, welcomed him home.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of Valmiki's Ramayana does the return flight aboard the Pushpaka Vimana primarily take place?
questions.explanation: The homeward journey in the Pushpaka is narrated in the Yuddha Kanda, after Ravana's defeat.

questions.q: According to tradition, after returning to Ayodhya, what did Rama eventually do with the Pushpaka Vimana?
options: Returned it so it went back to Kubera
Burned it in a great fire
questions.explanation: Rama returned the borrowed Pushpaka, and it went back to its rightful owner Kubera.

questions.q: Kubera, the original divine owner of the Pushpaka, is also known by which title?
options: Lord of wealth and the yakshas
questions.explanation: Kubera is the god of wealth and king of the yakshas, half-brother to Ravana.

questions.q: The Pushpaka was famed for obeying which command of its rider?
options: Going wherever the rider wished by thought
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka was a wish-directed chariot that traveled wherever its master desired.

questions.q: Which mother of Ravana's lineage connects him by blood to Kubera, the Pushpaka's owner?
options: They shared the father, the sage Vishrava
questions.explanation: Ravana and Kubera were half-brothers, both sons of the sage Vishrava by different mothers.

questions.q: During the Lanka war, where had the Pushpaka Vimana been kept by Ravana?
options: In a cave on Mount Trikuta's base
questions.explanation: Ravana kept the Pushpaka within his magnificent palace in the citadel of Lanka.

questions.q: The Pushpaka is repeatedly praised in the epic for its appearance as what?
options: A radiant, jewel-adorned celestial palace in flight
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes the Pushpaka as a dazzling, gem-encrusted aerial palace, brilliant as the sun.

questions.q: What does the Pushpaka Vimana most enduringly symbolize in the Ramayana's narrative of Rama's return?
options: The triumphant homecoming after exile and victory
The start of the forest exile
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka's flight to Ayodhya embodies the joyous, triumphant homecoming that culminates in Rama's coronation.
